stmt: ∀ (α : Type*) [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], (∀ x : α, ⊠x ≐ (⊤ : α)) → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α ≃ {x : α // x ≐ (⊤ : α)})
summary_delta: The proof effectively identifies Gödel fixed points with the elements equivalent to `⊤`, not just their existence. Making that identification explicit gives a clean interface theorem for the fixed-point subtype, which is the kind of exact classification bridge the current theory state is missing.
priority_rationale: This is the clean Gödel-subtype interface the frontier explicitly lacks, and it would upgrade pointwise collapse to an exact reusable classification.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], (∃ x : α, x ≤ ⊥ ∧ x ≐ ⊠x) → ACR.Inconsistent α
summary_delta: The solved result becomes more reusable when reframed as an obstruction theorem: any refutable reflection-fixed point already collapses the theory. This isolates the structural cause of inconsistency in a way that is stronger than the specific bottom instance and more informative than another global-collapse variant.
priority_rationale: It isolates inconsistency in reusable fixed-point language and would materially sharpen the current boundary between trivial `⊠` behavior and collapse.

stmt: ∀ (α : Type*) [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], (∀ x : α, ⊠x ≐ (⊤ : α)) → ((Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ x : α, x ≐ (⊤ : α)) ∧ ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, g.1 ≐ (⊤ : α))
summary_delta: The verified proof showed that consistency and universal `□`-fixedness are not used. Isolating the true hypothesis threshold turns the solved statement into a sharper structural criterion for the Gödel locus under constant-top reflection, which is more reusable than the original packaged theorem and directly advances the agenda’s preference for exact minimal assumptions.
priority_rationale: It sharpens an already verified constant-top theorem by removing extra assumptions, but it stops short of the subtype-level interface.

stmt: ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (_ : ACR.C5 α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ (∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, g.1 ≐ (⊤ : α)) ∧ ¬ (∀ x : α, ⊠x ≐ (⊤ : α))
summary_delta: A strong next step is to separate the solved sufficient condition from the actual fixed-point phenomenon. Such a counterexample would show that universal top-reflection is not necessary for all Gödel fixed points to collapse to `⊤`, sharpening the boundary between an extreme trivialization hypothesis and the broader canonical classification program.
priority_rationale: A separating counterexample would clarify necessity of universal top-reflection, though it is still boundary evidence rather than a positive interface theorem.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], (∀ x : α, ⊠x ≤ x) → ACR.Inconsistent α
summary_delta: The verified proof only used the direction ⊠⊥ ≤ ⊥, so the real boundary is one-sided deflationary reflection, not full equivalence. This gives a sharper minimal-threshold theorem and directly advances the theory-state goal of separating harmless global □-collapse from inconsistency-forcing ⊠-collapse.
priority_rationale: This is a plausible minimal-threshold inconsistency theorem for deflationary reflection and would sharpen a real bottleneck.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Reft α], (∀ x : α, x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)) → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α ≃ {x : α // x ≐ (⊤ : α)})
summary_delta: The proof logs show that the solved theorem is really driven by a constant-top reflection law, not by incidental details of the chosen canonical witness. Packaging that into a subtype-equivalence theorem would turn the current pointwise collapse result into the exact Gödel-locus interface the theory state is missing, and it would be reusable beyond the specific bounded-linear-order construction.
priority_rationale: This is another Gödel-locus subtype equivalence, but it overlaps heavily with the stronger constant-top interface pressure already in the queue.
